// note: Canonical MCP server definitions. ai.sh auto-registers only the lean always-on set (filesystem, context7). Everything else is opt-in via `mcp-toggle <name> on|off` (or `/mcp`) so the per-turn schema cost stays low — each heavy server adds 2k–10k context tokens every turn. github is opt-in because the `gh` CLI covers routine work; composio is registered by ai.sh only when COMPOSIO_API_KEY is set. Secrets come from env (~/.env), never inline.

github.// note: Opt-in (heavy schema, re-sent every turn). Enable with `github-on` / `mcp-toggle github on` when you need GitHub tools beyond the gh CLI.

context7.// note: Up-to-date, version-correct library docs into context. Low overhead. CONTEXT7_API_KEY is optional (raises rate limits).

composio.// note: Opt-in: 300+ SaaS/API connectors. Heavy on context tokens — registered by ai.sh only when COMPOSIO_API_KEY is set. Verify the start command against the current composio CLI.

playwright.// note: Opt-in (heavy: ~13.7k tool-def tokens at startup). Prefer the Agent Browser / Playwright CLI instead; enable with `browser-on` only when you need interactive MCP control.

chrome-devtools.// note: Opt-in. Network/console/performance/DOM debugging via CDP. Enable with `browser-on` when diagnosing runtime issues.
